---
title: ""
pagetitle: "DEN Economics Lab"
page-layout: full
toc: false
include-in-header:
  text: |
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
    <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@400;500;600;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
---

::: {.hero}
<span class="eyebrow">Research · Policy · Impact</span>

# DEN Economics Lab

<p class="lead">
The research arm of Indonesia's Dewan Ekonomi Nasional (National Economic Council).
We produce evidence-based analysis on macroeconomic, trade, and industrial policy
to inform decisions across government and the broader Indonesian economy.
</p>

[Explore Research](research.qmd){.btn .btn-primary .btn-lg}
[Meet the Team](people.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-light .btn-lg}
:::

::: {.stats}
::: {.stat}
[1+]{.stat-number}

[Researchers]{.stat-label}
:::
::: {.stat}
[1+]{.stat-number}

[Publications]{.stat-label}
:::
::: {.stat}
[1]{.stat-number}

[Active Projects]{.stat-label}
:::
::: {.stat}
[1+]{.stat-number}

[Partner Institutions]{.stat-label}
:::
:::

::: {.section-header}
## What We Do

[Rigorous research. Actionable insights.]{.subtitle}
:::

::: {.feature-grid}
::: {.feature-card}
### Macroeconomics

Fiscal, monetary, and growth diagnostics for the Indonesian economy. Structural
models including DSGE and heterogeneous-agent frameworks.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Trade & Industry

CGE modeling with GTAP-RD/GDyn-FS, tariff analysis, and applied work on trade
agreements (Indonesia–US ART, ASEAN integration).
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Applied Econometrics

Panel methods (PMG/MG/DFE), gravity estimation with `ppmlhdfe`, and modern
causal-inference designs applied to Indonesian micro and macro data.
:::

::: {.feature-card}
### Capacity Building

Training the next generation of Indonesian economists through workshops,
supervised research, and open-source tooling (`fig-den`, `ggden`).
:::
:::

::: {.section-header}
## Recent Publications

[Selected recent work from the lab]{.subtitle}
:::

::: {.publication}
[Trade Liberalisation and Household Welfare in Indonesia: Evidence from District-Level Panel Data]{.pub-title}

[Krisna Gupta, Dinda Rachmania, Arya Pratama]{.pub-authors}

[Journal of Development Economics · 2024]{.pub-meta}

[We exploit variation in tariff reductions to identify the causal effect of trade openness on household consumption and poverty rates across 500+ Indonesian districts from 2000–2020.]{.pub-abstract}

[Trade]{.tag} [Indonesia]{.tag} [Causal Inference]{.tag}
:::

::: {.publication}
[Fiscal Decentralisation, Local Governance Quality, and Public Service Delivery]{.pub-title}

[Siti Aminah, Budi Santoso, Krisna Gupta]{.pub-authors}

[World Development · 2024]{.pub-meta}

[Using a regression-discontinuity design around population thresholds that determine local government tiers, we estimate the effect of decentralisation on health and education outcomes.]{.pub-abstract}

[Fiscal Policy]{.tag} [Public Finance]{.tag} [RDD]{.tag}
:::

::: {.publication}
[Carbon Pricing in a Developing Economy: Labour Market and Distributional Effects]{.pub-title}

[Dinda Rachmania, Yudo Prabowo]{.pub-authors}

[Energy Policy · 2023]{.pub-meta}

[A CGE model calibrated to the Indonesian input-output table assesses how alternative carbon-pricing schemes affect wages, employment, and income distribution across sectors.]{.pub-abstract}

[Climate]{.tag} [CGE]{.tag} [Labour Economics]{.tag}
:::

::: {.text-center .mt-4}
[All publications](research.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
:::
